change_summary: EXP-196 integrated into WIN-001.detail_evidence: two new paragraphs ("second derivation is the same formula in disguise" and "same circuit, two incompatible wave speeds") inserted between the existing "dome formula exposes the relabeling" and "Tesla location destroys the dome interpretation" paragraphs. detail_evidence grew from 3677 chars (c3) to 6890 chars. No other WIN-001 field was modified. Decider also corrected anchor format from #win-029 (in EXP) to #win029 (matching live HTML anchor id) during integration.
our_argument_summary: WIN-001 still argues the 11.78 Hz derivation is a relabeled globe resonance, not a dome prediction. Post-EXP-196 the argument now additionally closes the "you attacked the wrong formula" defense (dome publishes both wins.html and live.html derivations; both reduce to the same Earth-sphere arithmetic by construction) and quantifies the cross-WIN speed swap between WIN-001 (v_a) and WIN-029 (c) on the dome's own "same aetheric circuit" claim. Verdict: Refuted by Data.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py / pull_data.py touches WIN-001. Both derivations (wins.html f = c/(2*disc_thickness); live.html f = v_a/(2*r_disc) with v_a=1.574c) are static HTML. The live-power dashboard row displays hardcoded Predicted=11.788 / Observed=11.7869 with 1% tolerance.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded. Predicted and observed values are static text.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 1.0% live-dashboard tolerance on a value known to four decimals since 1899.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The EXP-196 additions have been verified against raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 23 (v_a = 1.574c declared), line 58 (f = v_a/(2*r_disc) derivation), line 147 (r=0.999 "Same aetheric circuit"), line 52 (Schumann H = c/(4H0) using vacuum c). Raw-text confirms both the publication of two derivation forms and the per-WIN c-vs-v_a speed swap that paragraph B exploits.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-001. No API call, no live fetch, no automated comparison logic. The 11.787 Hz "match" is static HTML on wins.html plus a hardcoded live-power dashboard row.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Post-EXP-196, the relabeling argument is quantified in-situ: v_a = 1.574c is numerically within 0.2% of the spherical-cavity fundamental-mode phase velocity pi*c/2 = 1.5708c; r_disc = 20,015 km matches pi*R_earth = 20,015.09 km to four decimals (Earth's half-circumference under polar azimuthal-equidistant projection). Both constants are globe quantities relabeled as disc parameters. The live.html derivation reduces by construction to the wins.html derivation via the identity v_a/c = r_disc/disc_thickness = 1.574 = 20015/12717.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tesla's US Patent 787412 (1905) and Colorado Springs Notes (1899) predate the dome's "prediction" by over a century. The monitor script hardcodes the target value in the same commit as the data-fetch line, so temporal priority cannot be established from the repository.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Neither of the dome's two derivation paths uses a dome-intrinsic physical parameter. The wins.html path uses Earth's diameter (relabeled disc_thickness). The live.html path uses pi*R_earth and the spherical-cavity phase velocity pi*c/2 (relabeled r_disc and v_a). No dome-specific constant (firmament height H=9086 km, sun altitude 5733 km, moon altitude 2534 km, aetheric scale length) appears in either derivation.

holes_found.description: Paragraph A's formula-collapse chain is written as (pi*c/2)/(2*pi*R_earth) = c/(4*R_earth) = c/(2*D_earth) = "the wins.html formula in different variable names." A careful reader will notice that c/(2*D_earth) = c/(2*12742) = 11.76 Hz, whereas the wins.html formula c/(2*disc_thickness) = c/(2*12717) = 11.79 Hz. The two differ by 0.2% (exactly the 12717-vs-12742 gap already acknowledged elsewhere). The paragraph then does provide the tight ratio identity v_a/c = 1.574 = 20015/12717 = r_disc/disc_thickness which IS an exact by-construction collapse, but the reader must bridge from the loose pi-path collapse to the tight ratio-path collapse. A dome defender could exploit this by saying "your collapse produces 11.76 Hz, not 11.79 Hz, so the two formulas are NOT the same formula — they differ by exactly the 0.2% calibration constant I built into v_a."
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding a one-clause bridge, e.g. after the ratio identity: "and because v_a was fit to absorb the 0.2% offset between 2*R_earth and disc_thickness, the two forms agree at 11.787 Hz by construction, not by independent physics." This converts the two paths (pi-path and ratio-path) into one argument: v_a is tuned so that the pi-approximation chain closes exactly. Not a verdict-affecting hole; the paragraph's conclusion stands.

holes_found.description: Paragraph B quantifies the cross-WIN speed swap but does not preempt the dispersion-law defense in-situ. A dome defender will say: "Different modes on a dispersive medium can have different phase velocities — v_a for one mode, c for another, is physically reasonable." The EXP-196 anticipated_objections document addresses this ("no dispersion relation and no mode index is published"), but that material is not in the published paragraph. A reader who knows waveguide physics could supply the dispersion-law defense in their head and leave thinking paragraph B is less conclusive than it is.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider adding one sentence at the end of paragraph B: "The dome publishes no dispersion relation and no mode index that would justify the per-WIN speed selection, so the c-vs-v_a choice is operational, not theoretical." Optional — anticipated_objections carries this, but putting it in-line closes the rhetorical loop.

holes_found.description: Paragraph B's arithmetic "c / (2 * 20,015) = 7.49 Hz — far from 11.78 Hz" is correct (actual: 7.489 Hz, deviation -36.4%). The EXP's holes_addressed description characterized this as "38% low," which does not match the computed -36.4%. The paragraph itself uses "far from" rather than a number, so the published prose is safe; no patch needed. Flagging for tracker continuity — if anyone cites the "38%" figure downstream from EXP-196's commit message, they should use 36% instead.
holes_found.recommendation: No patch to wins.json. Tracker note only.

holes_found.description: Paragraph A cites "Snyder (1987)" (Map Projections—A Working Manual, USGS Prof. Paper 1395) for the polar azimuthal-equidistant projection identity pi*R_earth = half-circumference. The citation is accurate but bare: no DOI/URL, no page reference, no clickable link. A rigorous reader may want to verify. By comparison the rest of the detail_evidence uses explicit URLs (patents.google.com, live.html). Recommend upgrading to a hyperlink such as https://pubs.usgs.gov/pp/1395/ or noting "USGS Prof. Paper 1395, pp. 191-202" the standard AEP chapter.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish. If the reference is meant to do load-bearing work, give it a link; if it is just flavor, it is fine as is.

stronger_arguments.description: The ratio v_a/c = 1.574 equals 20015/12717 = 1.5740 to four figures — so v_a was chosen PRECISELY to make v_a * disc_thickness = c * r_disc. This is a four-significant-figure by-construction identity, not a physical coincidence. The paragraph hints at this but the strongest framing is: v_a is not derived, it is reverse-engineered from the disc_thickness / r_disc ratio. A one-line addition would turn the closing tell into the opening premise.
sources: Verified arithmetic: 20015/12717 = 1.57393 vs published v_a/c = 1.574 (five-figure agreement)

stronger_arguments.description: Paragraph B could strengthen the WIN-029 side by noting that WIN-029 ALSO claims post-hoc (H=9086 was chosen to roughly match c/(4*7.83)=9572 at 5.3% tolerance). The cross-check "apply v_a and H shifts to 15,066 km" destroys this post-hoc match by +66%, i.e. WIN-029 survives only at c; WIN-001 survives only at v_a. Neither has an independent derivation.
sources: WIN-029 current claim in data/wins.json (verdict: Self-Contradicted)

kernel_of_truth.description: The physical observation underneath paragraph A — that pi*c/2 is the fundamental-mode phase velocity of a spherical cavity resonator of Earth's radius, and that this quantity appears in ionospheric waveguide physics from the Watson (1919) era — is genuinely correct. It is an elegant observation about the Earth-ionosphere cavity. The dome correctly identified the numerical coincidence.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real spherical-cavity fact and then declaring it a flat-disc prediction does not make the prediction flat-disc. Paragraph A's contribution is to close the rhetorical loop the previous review left open: the live-page derivation, far from being a dome-parameter derivation, is the spherical-cavity derivation written with new variable names. Recognition of the kernel of truth strengthens rather than weakens the verdict.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender's strongest remaining play after EXP-196 is: "You have shown that v_a and r_disc numerically match globe constants, but numerical coincidence is not proof of relabeling — two correct theories can share numerical values. The dome claims that v_a=1.574c is derived from the aetheric medium constitutive equations and that r_disc is derived from the firmament-apex geometry; show me the work that says otherwise rather than asserting adoption." This is weaker than the pre-EXP defense because paragraph A now pins down the by-construction identity v_a/c = r_disc/disc_thickness — but it still requires the reader to take on faith that "by construction" means "not independently derived," and the dome has not been forced to publish the derivation of v_a or r_disc from first principles.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: None required; hole #1 (minor) is the only remaining rhetorical gap. The defense_survives score dropped from 4 (pre-EXP-196) to 2 because paragraphs A and B close the two major open flanks. Further strengthening (the stronger_arguments items above) would move it to 1 but is not load-bearing.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Paragraph B now codifies the v_a-vs-c cross-WIN inconsistency between WIN-001 (uses v_a=1.574c) and WIN-029 (uses c in H=c/(4f)) on the dome's own "same aetheric circuit" claim. Applying v_a consistently to WIN-029 shifts H to ~15,066 km (+66% vs published H=9086 km); applying c consistently to WIN-001 via f=c/(2*r_disc) shifts Tesla's frequency to 7.49 Hz (-36.4% vs 11.78 Hz). Neither WIN survives speed consistency. The WIN-002 selective-damping argument (detail_extra) remains orthogonal and is not touched by EXP-196. Note: an earlier c3-era hole ("detail_extra triplication") was addressed separately by the decider; the current detail_extra contains exactly one copy of the "selective damping signal" paragraph (verified in this c4).

claims_checked: pi*R_earth = 20015.0868 km vs claimed 20015.09 km (4-decimal match confirmed)

(pi*c/2)/(2*pi*R_earth) = 11.7639 Hz (computed via c/(4*R_earth)) — this is the collapse target. Equals c/(2*D_earth)=c/(2*12742)=11.7639. NOT equal to wins.html c/(2*12717)=11.7871 Hz. Delta 0.2% — same 0.2% as the 12717-vs-12742 gap; see hole #1.
(11.78-7.49)/11.78 = 36.4% — claimed "38% low" in EXP holes_addressed is off (see hole #3 minor); paragraph B does NOT publish the 38% figure, uses "far from" instead, so publication is safe
quantitative_verification.computations: python3 arithmetic with c=299792.458 km/s, R_earth=6371 km. All paragraph A and paragraph B published numbers match to rounding. The EXP metadata's "38% low" characterization is slightly inaccurate (actual -36.4%) but this figure was not published.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: EXP metadata holes_addressed section says Tesla frequency shifts to "38% low" when applying c; actual shift is -36.4%. Does not appear in the published paragraph (which says only "far from 11.78 Hz") — no patch required.

deeper_analysis: EXP-196 converts WIN-001 from a defensible-with-effort Refuted-by-Data verdict to a much harder target for a dome defender. Before EXP-196 the c3 review identified two major open flanks: (a) the dome's live.html form f=v_a/(2*r_disc) was never engaged, and (b) the WIN-001/WIN-029 c-vs-v_a speed swap across the dome's claimed "same aetheric circuit" was never stated in-line. Paragraphs A and B close both flanks with explicit arithmetic and direct citation to raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 23, 58, 52, 147. The remaining holes are all minor: a 0.2% rhetorical gap in paragraph A's collapse chain (the pi-path gives c/(2*D_earth)=c/(2*12742)=11.76 Hz while the wins.html formula uses 12717, so the phrase "the wins.html formula in different variable names" is not strictly accurate, though the subsequent ratio identity IS exact); a missed preemption of the dispersion-law defense in paragraph B (noted in EXP anticipated_objections but not in the paragraph); a Snyder (1987) citation without a link; and a -36.4% vs -38% rounding slip in the EXP metadata that is not visible on the site. None of these change the verdict or the argument's structural integrity. The post-EXP-196 WIN-001 is one of the more defensively complete WINs in the review, and the defense_survives score drops from 4 to 2. Recommended action: no_change; the minor polish items above are optional.

citations_verified: US Patent 787412 (patents.google.com/patent/US787412A) — link in WIN-001 body, patent text confirms 471,240 km/s and 0.08484 s quotes

live.html page (john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html) — cited twice in EXP-196 paragraphs; raw-text/08-live-power.txt confirms the v_a=1.574c declaration (line 23), f=v_a/(2*r_disc) form (line 58), c/(4H0) form (line 52), and r=0.999 "Same aetheric circuit" claim (line 147)
Dome evolution page circular-reasoning admission (raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 36) — unchanged from c3
citations_unchecked: Snyder (1987) Map Projections—A Working Manual — bare citation without URL (minor hole #4). The reference is accurate in its bibliographic details (USGS Prof. Paper 1395) and the polar azimuthal equidistant projection does indeed map the antipode to pi*R_earth from the origin, so the claim it supports is correct — but the citation itself is not hyperlinked.
